title: Distillation teacher/student PEFT training pipeline (CLI invocation; produces LoRA artifact)
goal: Implement the teacher/student PEFT distillation pipeline: takes a TrainingCorpus (from MT-119) + teacher ModelId + student base ModelId; runs PEFT LoRA training (out-of-process Python invocation via PEFT/Transformers since Rust has no production PEFT trainer yet); produces a distilled LoRA artifact mountable into ModelRuntime per spec §4.8 + AC-DISTILL-EDITABLE-SURFACE (note: training is not the editable surface in V0; AC-DISTILL-EDITABLE-SURFACE caps V0 to ModelManual + retrieval policy params).
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/src/distillation/peft_pipeline.rs orchestrating the subprocess Python invocation; new scripts/distill/train_lora.py (Python PEFT training script); ProcessOwnershipLedger row.
implementation_notes: Per spec §4.8 + refinement INF-1 (LoRA-as-distillation-output). PEFT training is not available in Rust at production maturity in 2026 — the practical path is to call out to Python PEFT/Transformers via subprocess. PeftPipeline::distill(teacher_id, student_base_id, corpus, hyperparams) -> Result<DistilledLoraArtifact, DistillError>: (1) ContentReview::review each turn (gate); (2) write filtered corpus to JSONL on disk (governed artifact); (3) spawn Python subprocess `python scripts/distill/train_lora.py --corpus <jsonl> --teacher <path> --student <path> --out <lora_dir> --rank 16 --epochs 1 --lr 2e-4` with sandbox boundary (SandboxAdapter from cluster B for the python process); (4) register subprocess via ProcessOwnershipLedger with engine_kind=DistillationJob; (5) await completion + parse the output LoRA safetensors + metadata; (6) wrap as DistilledLoraArtifact { lora_id: v7, safetensors_path, target_modules, rank, base_model_compat, teacher_model_id, corpus_size, trained_at_utc, license_tag (inherited from teacher), provenance: { teacher_id, student_base_id, corpus_hash, hyperparams_hash } }; (7) DistilledLoraArtifact is mountable into ModelRuntime via LoraStackOps from MT-064. NOT EDITABLE-SURFACE in V0 (AC-DISTILL-EDITABLE-SURFACE explicitly excludes LoRA weights from V0 editable surface) — this pipeline produces a candidate; promotion to active is operator-driven via PromotionGate (separate from V0 self-improvement loop). Python script is opt-in extra (`scripts/distill/requirements.txt`: torch, peft, transformers, datasets); operator MUST provision Python env (documented in tests/README.json under "distillation_python_env"). Tests (env-gated by HANDSHAKE_TEST_PYTHON_DISTILL=1): tiny synthetic corpus + tiny student model -> training completes + LoRA artifact produced + mountable. Non-Python-env test: pipeline returns Err with explicit "python_env_not_provisioned" message.
risk_if_missed: Distillation output produces nothing mountable; AC-DISTILL-EDITABLE-SURFACE constraint observed but no pipeline path.
validator_focus: Subprocess sandboxed via SandboxAdapter; ProcessOwnershipLedger row; LoRA mountable; license inherited; V0 NOT editable-surface (this is a candidate-producing pipeline, not the self-improvement loop).

minimum_controls: LoRA artifact mountable via MT-064 LoraStackOps (round-trip test).
